tasks.label: My share of the group project
Teammate's share (just dumped on me)

memory.summary: Teammate A offered to help with their part, suggesting I take it over since I'm better at it. This contradicts my plan to finish my own share this afternoon.
Teammate A now directly requests help in chat, and unexpectedly took on my own workload. My energy is dropping while stress rises.
Teammate A's willingness to split work or defer shows trust isn't broken by setting boundaries. Their direct, polite response confirms a healthy dynamic despite the workload shift.
Teammate A unexpectedly took my workload despite initial offers, shifting the dynamic. My energy is dropping while stress rises due to this sudden change.
The deadline passed with my progress incomplete. I must now address this unfinished task while managing rising stress.
After working two hours, my progress increased slightly to 0.62, but the deadline passed without completion. My stress remains high as I now need to finish this task.
Despite low energy and high stress, I successfully advanced my project progress from 0.62 to 0.67 in 1.5 hours, confirming continued work is possible.
I worked 2 hours to raise project progress from 0.67 to 0.73, confirming that continued effort is possible despite low energy and high stress.
I worked 2 hours, raising progress from 0.73 to 0.79 despite low energy and high stress. This confirms continued effort is possible even when fatigued.
Progress increased to 0.82 in one hour, confirming that work remains feasible even with high stress and sleep debt.
Progress jumped to 0.88 in two hours, proving sustained work is possible even with high stress and sleep debt.
Progress reached 0.92 in 1.5 hours, further confirming sustained work is possible despite high stress and sleep debt.
